content: Ari flagged in eng standup that Snowflake query costs spiked 40% YoY. Root cause: vector embedding pipeline running unoptimized on staging. Anya will audit monthly spend; I'm owning vendor review process.
summary: Snowflake costs spiked 40%. Ari identified unoptimized embeddings.

content: Sebastien scheduled 3 back-to-back 1:1s with zero notice. I was deep in Q4 budget. Texted him after: 'async first unless urgent.' He acknowledged. Reset: calendar blocked for async blocks now.
summary: Push back on surprise meetings. Establish async-first default.

content: Decision: Switched from Notion to Linear for ops tracking. Reason: Notion queries were stale, Linear integrates with GitHub/eng workflows. Cost ~$2k/yr. Documented decision in Slack thread #ops-tooling.
summary: Migrated ops tracking from Notion to Linear.

content: Realized I got sharp with Jenny in async feedback. She'd missed context from an earlier Lina/Marcus discussion. I wrote a 2-min video walkthrough instead of repeating myself. Felt patronizing. Need better org memory.
summary: Frustration when re-explaining. Video helps; still drains me.

content: Anya asked me to join a vendor call with Anthropic unprepped. I declined, said 'send me a one-pager first.' She bristled initially, then said it saved us because I caught licensing ambiguity she'd missed.
summary: Prep first. No cold calls. Prevents wasted cycles.

content: Marcus needed Q1 hiring plan for CS. I gave him a spreadsheet instead of a meeting. He said it was clearer than 3 previous sync convos combined. Validated my async preference again.
Process established: all decisions ≥ $5k require written proposal (Linear ticket, 3-line rationale, stakeholder approval). Sebastien, Anya, and Lina sign off async. Reduces decision lag by ~3 days.
summary: Decision gate: written proposal + async sign-off for $5k+.

content: Tom shipped a Sentry integration without telling DevOps. Alerts went haywire. Took 2 hours to trace. Debrief: communicated in eng Slack, got buried. I added 'ops-critical changes' Slack channel + weekly async digest.
summary: Tom's Sentry change broke visibility. Formalized notification process.

content: Priya asked me to interview a recruiting candidate. I said no—not my function. But I wrote her a note on what ops candidates should know about our infra. Boundaries + value add.
summary: Say no clearly. Help where you own the skill.

content: AWS bill analysis: Cloudflare DDoS protection redundant with AWS Shield Advanced. Consolidating saves $8.5k/yr. Documented in Anya's cost ledger. Action: migrate rules by Feb 15.
summary: Cloudflare + AWS Shield overlap. Cutting Cloudflare.

content: Caught myself about to schedule a 30-min sync to discuss an Acme Corp contract issue. Stopped. Wrote a 200-word async brief instead. Anya replied in 4 hours with solution. Why do I ever default to meetings?
summary: Muscle memory: reach for sync. Async faster. Why?

content: Devon asked why I document every vendor decision. I said: 'So in 6 months when you ask again, I don't re-explain. So Sebastien doesn't re-decide.' He said I sound like he's broken. Maybe I am. But it works.
summary: Meticulous documentation = no re-work. Sounds mean. Is efficient.

content: James onboarded last week. Anya scheduled 4 sync sessions with him. I intervened: 'Use the ops wiki + async Q&A.' He's progressing faster than Tom. Feels validated, but also cold. Wondering if I've optimized myself into isolation.
summary: Efficiency can feel isolating. James succeeding anyway.

content: Initiated vendor audit: 27 active accounts. Consolidation targets: DataDog + Sentry overlap (observability), GitHub + 1Password (identity). Potential savings $14.2k/yr. Presented to Anya. Greenlit for March.

Lina asked in Slack why we switched Linear. I realized I'd never told Product. She felt out of loop. Sent her full decision doc retroactively. She said it was fine, but I felt the sting. Better comms needed upstream.
summary: Lina unaware of Linear migration. Comms gap.

content: Sebastien said in 1:1 'you're the most organized person I know, but sometimes you're hard to reach.' Hit me. I'm efficient and defensive. Async protects me from interruption *and* from connection. Working on balance.
summary: Efficiency can become a wall. Sebastien noticed.
